name: Fudgy Hanukkah Cake
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------ICING-----------------------------------
1/4 cup Cocoa powder; unsweetened - sifted
4 tablespoons Sugar; granulated
4 tablespoons Cornstarch
1/2 cup Milk; skim
1/4 cup Corn syrup
1 teaspoon Vanilla
Edible gold glitter; optional
-or coconut
In a bowl, combine brown sugar, flour, cocoa, baking soda and baking powder, cinnamon, and salt. In a separate bowl, beat together egg and egg white; then beat in buttermilk, coffee, and oil. Make a well in the sugar mixture; pour the coffee mixture into the well and whisk just until blended. Pour into greased 8-inch square cake pans. Bake in a 350°F oven for 30 to 40 minutes or until a tester inserted into the centre comes out clean and the cake springs back when pressed. Let cool in the pan on a rack for 20 minutes. (Cake can be frozen up to 2 months) ICING: In a small saucepan, combine cocoa, sugar, and cornstarch. Whisk in milk until blended, then add corn syrup and vanilla. Bring to boil over medium-high heat, whisking constantly, then reduce heat to low and cook, whisking for 1 to 2 minutes or until thickened and glossy. Let cool for 5 minutes. Spread evenly over cake. Sprinkle with glitter if using. SERVES: 12
